volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 1347 - Section 1445)

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: ἐνθύμιόν μοι τότε τίθησι Λοξίας ⋯
sections.translation: Loxias put in my heart that day —

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δρᾶσαι ; λέγε , πέραινε σοὺς λόγους .
sections.translation: What purpose ? Oh ! speak , finish thy story .

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: σῶσαι τόδʼ εὕρημʼ ἐς τὸν ὄντα νῦν χρόνον .
sections.translation: To preserve what I had found until the present time .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ἔχει δέ μοι τί κέρδος ⋯ ἢ τίνα βλάβην ;
sections.translation: What weal or woe doth this import to me ?

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: ἐνθάδε κέκρυπται σπάργανʼ οἷς ἐνῆσθα σύ .
sections.translation: Herein were laid the swaddling-clothes in which thou wert enwrapped .

sections.label: Section 1352
sections.original: μητρὸς τάδʼ ἡμῖν ἐκφέρεις ζητήματα ;
sections.translation: These relics thou art producing may help me to find my mother .

sections.label: Section 1353
sections.original: ἐπεί γʼ ὁ δαίμων βούλεται · πάροιθε δʼ οὔ .
sections.translation: Yes , for now the deity so wills it , though not before .

sections.label: Section 1354
sections.original: ὦ μακαρίων μοι φασμάτων ἥδʼ ἡμέρα .
sections.translation: Hail ! thou day of visions blest to me !

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: λαβών νυν αὐτὰ τὴν τεκοῦσαν ἐκπόνει .
sections.translation: Take then the relics and seek thy mother diligently .

sections.label: Section 1356
sections.original: πᾶσάν γʼ ἐπελθὼν Ἀσιάδʼ Εὐρώπης θʼ ὅρους .
sections.translation: [*] And when thou hast traversed Asia and the bounds of Europe ,

sections.label: Section 1357
sections.original: γνώσῃ τάδʼ αὐτός . τοῦ θεοῦ δʼ ἕκατί σε
sections.translation: thou wilt learn this for thyself ; for the god ’ s sake I reared thee , my child , and now to thee do I entrust these relics , which he willed that I should take

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: σῶσαί θʼ · ὅτου δʼ ἐβούλεθʼ οὕνεκʼ , οὐκ ἔχω λέγειν .
sections.translation: into my safe keeping , without being bidden ; why he willed it I cannot tell thee . For no living soul wist that I had them in my possession , nor yet their hiding-place . And now farewell ! as a mother might her child , so I greet thee . The starting-point of thy inquiry for thy mother must be this ;

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν εἴ τις Δελφίδων τεκοῦσά σε
sections.translation: first , was it a Delphian maid that gave birth to thee , and exposed thee in this temple ; next , was it a daughter of Hellas at all ? That is all that I and Phoebus , who shares in thy lot , can do for thee . [ Exit Pythian Priestess .

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · κατʼ ὄσσων ὡς ὑγρὸν βάλλω δάκρυ ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! the tears stream from my eyes

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: ἐκεῖσε τὸν νοῦν δούς , ὅθʼ ἡ τεκοῦσά με
sections.translation: when I think of the day my mother bore me , as the fruit of her secret love , only to smuggle her babe away privily , without suckling it ; nameless I led a servant ’ s life in the courts of the god . His service truly was kindly , yet was my fortune

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: βαρέα · χρόνον γὰρ ὅν με χρῆν ἐν ἀγκάλαις
sections.translation: heavy ; for just when I ought to have lain softly in a mother ’ s arms , tasting somewhat of the joys of life , was I deprived of a fond mother ’ s fostering care . Nor less is she a prey to sorrow that bare me , seeing she hath suffered the self-same pang in losing all the joy a son might bring .

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: καὶ νῦν λαβὼν τήνδʼ ἀντίπηγʼ οἴσω θεῷ
sections.translation: Now will I take and bear this ark unto the god as an offering , that herein I may discover naught that I would rather not . For if haply my mother proves to be some slave-girl , ’ twere worse to find her out than let her rest in silence . O ! Phoebus , to thy temple do I dedicate this ark .

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: καίτοι τί πάσχω ; τοῦ θεοῦ προθυμίᾳ
sections.translation: Yet why ? this is to war against the god ’ s intention , who saved these tokens of my mother for my sake . I must undo the lid and bear the worst . For that which fate ordains , I may ne ’ er o ’ erstep . O ! hallowed wreaths and fastenings ,

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: καὶ σύνδεθʼ , οἷσι τἄμʼ ἐφρουρήθη φίλα ;
sections.translation: that have kept so safe these relics dear to me ; why , ah ! why were ye hidden from me ? Behold the covering of this rounded ark ! No signs of age are here , owing to some miracle ; decay hath not touched these chaplets ; and yet ’ tis long enough since these were stored away .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: τί δῆτα φάσμα τῶν ἀνελπίστων ὁρῶ ;
sections.translation: Ha ! what unlooked-for sight is here ?

sections.label: Section 1396
sections.original: σίγα σύ · πολλὰ καὶ πάροιθεν οἶσθά μοι ⋯
sections.translation: Peace , woman ! now , as erst , thou art my enemy .

sections.label: Section 1397
sections.original: οὐκ ἐν σιωπῇ τἀμά · μή με νουθέτει .
sections.translation: Silence is not for me . Bid me not be still ; for lo ! I see the ark wherein I did expose thee , my child , in days gone by , whilst yet a tender babe in the cavern of

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: Κέκροπος ἐς ἄντρα καὶ Μακρὰς πετρηρεφεῖς .
sections.translation: Cecrops , ’ neath the rocky roof of Macrae . So now will I leave this altar , though death await me .

sections.label: Section 1402
sections.original: λάζυσθε τήνδε · θεομανὴς γὰρ ἥλατο
sections.translation: Seize her ; she is mad , springing thus from the shelter of the carved altar . Bind her arms .

sections.label: Section 1404
sections.original: σφάζοντες οὐ λήγοιτʼ ἄν · ὡς ἀνθέξομαι
sections.translation: Kill ! spare not ! for I to thee will cleave ,

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: καὶ τῆσδε καὶ σοῦ τῶν τε σῶν κεκρυμμένων .
sections.translation: and to this ark , and all that is within it .

sections.label: Section 1406
sections.original: τάδʼ οὐχὶ δεινά ; ῥυσιάζομαι λόγῳ .
sections.translation: Is not this monstrous ? here am I laid claim to on a specious pretext .

sections.label: Section 1407
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ σοῖς φίλοισιν εὑρίσκῃ φίλος .
sections.translation: Nay , nay , but as a friend art thou by friends now found .

sections.label: Section 1408
sections.original: ἐγὼ φίλος σός ; κᾆτά μʼ ἔκτεινες λάθρα ;
sections.translation: I a friend of thine ! and wouldst thou , then , have slain me privily ?

sections.label: Section 1409
sections.original: παῖς γʼ , εἰ τόδʼ ἐστὶ τοῖς τεκοῦσι φίλτατον .
sections.translation: Thou art my child , if that is what a parent holds most dear .

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: παῦσαι πλέκουσα . — λήψομαί σʼ ἐγὼ καλῶς .
sections.translation: An end to thy web of falsehood ! Right well will I convict thee .

sections.label: Section 1411
sections.original: ἐς τοῦθʼ ἱκοίμην , τοῦδε τοξεύω , τέκνον .
sections.translation: My child , that is my aim ; God grant I reach it !

sections.label: Section 1412
sections.original: κενὸν τόδʼ ἄγγος ἢ στέγει πλήρωμά τι ;
sections.translation: Is this ark empty , or hath it aught within ?

sections.label: Section 1413
sections.original: σά γʼ ἔνδυθʼ , οἷσί σʼ ἐξέθηκʼ ἐγώ ποτε .
sections.translation: Thy raiment wherein I exposed thee long ago .

sections.label: Section 1414
sections.original: καὶ τοὔνομʼ αὐτῶν ἐξερεῖς πρὶν εἰσιδεῖν ;
sections.translation: Wilt put a name thereto before thou see it ?

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: κἂν μὴ φράσω γε , κατθανεῖν ὑφίσταμαι .
sections.translation: Unless I describe it , I offer to die .

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: λέγʼ · ὡς ἔχει τι δεινὸν ἥ γε τόλμα σου .
sections.translation: Say on ; there is something strange in this thy confidence .

sections.label: Section 1417
sections.original: σκέψασθʼ · ὃ παῖς ποτʼ οὖσʼ ὕφασμʼ ὕφηνʼ ἐγὼ ⋯
sections.translation: Behold the robe my childish fingers wove .

sections.label: Section 1418
sections.original: ποῖόν τι ; πολλὰ παρθένων ὑφάσματα .
sections.translation: Describe it ; maidens weave many a pattern .

sections.label: Section 1419
sections.original: οὐ τέλεον , οἷον δʼ ἐκδίδαγμα κερκίδος .
sections.translation: ’ Tis not perfect , but a first lesson , as it were , in weaving .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: μορφὴν ἔχον τίνʼ ; ὥς με μὴ ταύτῃ λάβῃς .
sections.translation: Describe its form ; thou shalt not catch me thus .

sections.label: Section 1421
sections.original: Γοργὼ μὲν ἐν μέσοισιν ἠτρίοις πέπλων .
sections.translation: A Gorgon figures in the centre of the warp .

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τίς ἡμᾶς ἐκκυνηγετεῖ πότμος ;
sections.translation: Great Zeus ! what fate is this that dogs my steps ?

sections.label: Section 1423
sections.original: κεκρασπέδωται δʼ ὄφεσιν αἰγίδος τρόπον .
sections.translation: ’ Tis fringed with snakes like an aegis .

sections.label: Section 1424
sections.original: ἰδού ·
sections.translation: Lo ! ’ tis the very robe ; how true we find the voice of God !

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: ὦ χρόνιον ἱστῶν παρθένευμα τῶν ἐμῶν .
sections.translation: Ah ! woven work that erst my virgin shuttle wrought .

sections.label: Section 1426
sections.original: ἔστιν τι πρὸς τῷδʼ , ἢ μόνῳ τῷδʼ εὐτυχεῖς ;
sections.translation: Is there aught beside , or stays thy lucky guessing here ?

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original: δράκοντες · ἀρχαῖόν τι παγχρύσῳ γένει
sections.translation: There be serpents , too , with jaws of gold , an old-world symbol .

sections.label: Section 1428
sections.original: δώρημʼ Ἀθάνας , ἣ τέκνʼ ἐντρέφειν λέγει ⋯
sections.translation: [*] Is that Athena ’ s gift , bidding her race grow up under their guardianship ?

sections.label: Section 1429
sections.original: Ἐριχθονίου γε τοῦ πάλαι μιμήματα .
sections.translation: Yes , to copy our ancestor Erichthonius .

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: τί δρᾶν , τί χρῆσθαι , φράζε μοι , χρυσώματι ;
sections.translation: What is their object ? what the use of these golden gauds ? pray , tell .

sections.label: Section 1431
sections.original: δέραια παιδὶ νεογόνῳ φέρειν , τέκνον .
sections.translation: Necklaces for the new-born babe to wear , my child .

sections.label: Section 1432
sections.original: ἔνεισιν οἵδε · τὸ δὲ τρίτον ποθῶ μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: Lo ! here they lie . Yet would I know the third sign .

sections.label: Section 1433
sections.original: στέφανον ἐλαίας ἀμφέθηκά σοι τότε ,
sections.translation: About thy brow I bound an olive-wreath that day , plucked from the tree Athena first made grow on her own rock .

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ὅς , εἴπερ ἔστιν , οὔποτʼ ἐκλείπει χλόην ,
sections.translation: If haply that is there , it hath not lost its verdure yet , but still is fresh , for it came from the stock that grows not old .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτη μοι μῆτερ , ἄσμενός σʼ ἰδὼν
sections.translation: Mother , dearest mother , with what rapture I behold thee , as on thy cheeks , that share my joy , I press my lips !

sections.label: Section 1439
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ὦ φῶς μητρὶ κρεῖσσον ἡλίου —
sections.translation: My son , light that in thy mother ’ s eye outshinest yonder sun ,—

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: συγγνώσεται γὰρ ὁ θεός — ἐν χεροῖν σʼ ἔχω ,
sections.translation: I know the god will pardon me ,— in my arms I hold thee , whom I never hoped to find , for I thought thy home was in that nether world , among the ghosts with Queen Persephone .

sections.label: Section 1443
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ φίλη μοι μῆτερ , ἐν χεροῖν σέθεν
sections.translation: Ah , dear mother mine ! within thy arms I rest , the dead now brought to light , and dead no more .

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ , λαμπρᾶς αἰθέρος ἀμπτυχαί ,
sections.translation: Hail , thou broad expanse of bright blue sky ! What words can I find to utter my joy aloud ? Whence comes to me such unexpected rapture ? To what do I owe this bliss ?
